title: carmichaelia torulosa
intro: Carmichaelia torulosa, also known as the white pea bush, is a perennial legume native to New Zealand. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Carmichaelia torulosa include reducing inflammation, treating wounds, and alleviating fever. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies.
